metadata.title: MedVault System Security Plan

parties.name: Jose Rodriguez
parties.remarks: Project author and acting ISSO for portfolio purposes

metadata.remarks: Portfolio demonstration artifact. The MedVault system is fictional; no real federal authorization is represented.

system-characteristics.description: A federal civilian SaaS API for state and local health departments to submit jurisdiction-level public-health reports. The system ingests structured surveillance data, encrypts it at rest using AWS KMS customer-managed keys, and exposes audit-grade access logs for compliance evidence.

information-types.title: Public Health Surveillance Records
information-types.description: Jurisdiction-level health indicator counts submitted by state and local public health departments. Aggregate, non-identifying surveillance data.

status.remarks: Portfolio demonstration. No real federal operations.

authorization-boundary.description: The MedVault authorization boundary includes: (a) AWS S3 buckets (reports and access-logs) in us-east-1; (b) the AWS KMS customer-managed key encrypting bucket contents; (c) the GitHub Container Registry hosting the signed application image; (d) the GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines (terraform-scan, container-scan); (e) the FastAPI application packaged in a hardened distroless container. The boundary excludes the AWS control plane itself (inherited from AWS FedRAMP authorization) and the GitHub platform itself (inherited from GitHub's own attestations).

users.title: Terraform Deployment User
users.description: AWS IAM user with resource-scoped permissions (s3:* and curated KMS actions on medvault-* resources only). Used by Terraform from local developer workstations to deploy and manage MedVault infrastructure.

users.title: github-actions[bot]
users.description: GitHub Actions service identity. Authenticates to GHCR via short-lived GITHUB_TOKEN, signs container images via Sigstore Fulcio OIDC, commits OSCAL Assessment Results back to main.

components.title: MedVault Terraform/AWS Infrastructure
components.description: Imported from component-definitions/terraform-aws.json. See that document for control implementation detail.

components.title: MedVault GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
components.description: Imported from component-definitions/github-actions-ci.json. Two workflows providing continuous compliance enforcement.

components.title: MedVault FastAPI Application
components.description: Imported from component-definitions/fastapi-app.json. Distroless containerized FastAPI app.

control-implementation.description: Implementation claims for the 13 controls actively implemented by MedVault components. Remaining FedRAMP Moderate controls are either inherited from AWS (for cloud-platform controls) or planned for future phases (documented in the POA&M and roadmap).

by-components.description: Resource-scoped IAM least privilege. The Terraform user has s3:* + curated KMS actions scoped to medvault-* resources only. Cannot enumerate or modify resources outside the medvault namespace.
Runtime least privilege via distroless 'nonroot' base image. Container runs as UID 65532:65532, cannot escalate to root or write to system paths.
S3 server access logging enabled on the reports bucket, delivered to a separate access-logs bucket for tamper-resistance.
Structured JSON logs from the FastAPI app include timestamp, level, message, and request_id correlation ID propagated via ContextVar middleware.
Automated control assessments generated per CI run by scripts/evidence-to-oscal.py, transforming SARIF output into OSCAL Assessment Results, committed back to the repository.
Continuous monitoring via CI workflows triggered on every push. Both terraform-scan and container-scan pipelines re-assess on each change, producing fresh OSCAL evidence.

Workflow gate enforces policy: Checkov violations exit non-zero. GitHub branch protection (configured outside OSCAL) provides actual merge enforcement. Marked partial because branch protection is not yet formally screenshotted as evidence.
Configuration baseline enforced by Checkov: KMS encryption mandatory, public access blocked, TLS-only bucket policy, versioning enabled. Non-compliant Terraform cannot merge.
Distroless base image. No shell, no apt, no curl, no package manager in the final image. Multi-stage build discards builder stage.
Two scanners on every push: Checkov for IaC, Trivy for containers. SARIF to GitHub Security tab plus OSCAL Assessment Results for compliance evidence.
Developer security testing integrated into CI: Checkov SAST for IaC, Trivy composition analysis for container dependencies.
AWS KMS customer-managed key with annual rotation, SSE-KMS on data bucket. Access logs bucket uses AES256 to avoid circular dependency. POAM-001 tracks adding an explicit key policy.
Cosign keyless signing of every container image. Signature tied to workflow file at specific commit. Anchored in Sigstore Rekor public transparency log. Independently verifiable by anyone.

Provenance via Cosign keyless signing. Each signing event published to Sigstore Rekor transparency log. The signature certificate identity binds to the exact GitHub Actions workflow that produced the image.
